page: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dldsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with two threads per core. The processor is equipped with a 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It includes advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which accelerate vector and matrix operations.

The instance is configured with 192.0 GB of RAM, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. This low-memory profile represents a clear tradeoff, prioritizing raw compute density over memory capacity. For storage, the server is bundled with 5669 GB of local NVMe storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. No public IPv4 addresses are included by default.

In benchmark evaluations, the D96lds_v7 demonstrates strong multi-core performance, achieving top-tier results in PassMark CPU tests, including extended instructions, encryption, and physics. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small and large block transfers ranking in the top 10%, though the overall PassMark memory score remains in the average tier. The server also excels in data processing, showing top-tier performance in Redis SET operations, static web serving throughput, and single-threaded gzip compression. For machine learning, the hardware-accelerated AMX instructions enable top-tier LLM prompt processing speeds across small, medium, and large models, though text generation speeds fall into the average to strong ranges.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D96lds_v7 offers high compute and storage density relative to its memory footprint, making it a cost-effective choice for workloads that do not require massive RAM allocations but demand substantial processing power and local storage. It is optimized for high-throughput database operations, web serving, multi-threaded data decompression, and CPU-based machine learning inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dldsv7 family, powered by a 96-vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 48 physical cores and a 480 MB L3 cache. It features 192.0 GB of RAM, establishing a low-memory ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, and includes 5669 GB of local storage. Built on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in PassMark CPU tests, Redis SET operations, and LLM prompt processing, alongside high memory read bandwidth. However, its memory capacity is a key tradeoff, making it appropriate for compute-heavy and storage-reliant workloads rather than memory-intensive applications. It is optimized for high-throughput databases, web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and 5669 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier CPU and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v7 is a compute-optimized VM with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 5669 GB local storage for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute and storage virtual machine featuring advanced Intel matrix acceleration and a low memory-to-core ratio for optimized processing.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs with AVX-512 and AMX instruction sets
192 GB system memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
Top-tier performance in database operations and Redis SET benchmarks
Accelerated CPU-based LLM prompt processing via Intel AMX
Optimized for high-throughput web serving and compute-heavy workloads
